text: Electrical installation and repair is a high-demand skilled trade in Uganda, driven by rapid construction and the government's rural electrification programme. Startup costs: basic tool kit — wire strippers, pliers, screwdrivers, multimeter, cable cutters, insulation tape (UGX 200,000-400,000), safety gear — insulated gloves, boots, goggles (UGX 100,000-200,000), initial cable and accessories stock (UGX 100,000-300,000), UEGCL certification or apprenticeship completion certificate. Total startup: UGX 300,000-1,000,000. Training: 6-12 month apprenticeship under a master electrician (often free in exchange for labour), or BTVET certificate from a technical institute (1-2 years). Many successful electricians are self-taught through apprenticeship and YouTube tutorials.

Services and revenue model for electricians: House wiring (new construction): UGX 150,000-500,000 per house depending on size, takes 2-5 days. Materials (cables, sockets, switches, DB board) charged separately — typical markup 15-25%. Repair and fault-finding: UGX 30,000-100,000 per visit. Generator installation and maintenance: UGX 50,000-200,000 per job. Electrical appliance repair (fans, irons, cookers): UGX 10,000-50,000 per item. Solar panel installation (a massive growth area): UGX 100,000-300,000 labour for home systems, materials separate. CCTV and security system installation: UGX 100,000-500,000 per job. Average daily income: UGX 50,000-100,000 with steady work. Monthly potential: UGX 1,000,000-3,000,000. Busiest periods: dry season (October-February) when most construction happens.

Solar energy is the biggest growth opportunity for electricians in Uganda. Only 24% of Uganda's population has access to grid electricity, but solar can reach anywhere. Government programmes: Rural Electrification Agency (REA) contracts for community solar installations. Off-grid solar companies like M-KOPA, Fenix, and d.light need local installation technicians (pay UGX 30,000-50,000 per installation). Home solar kits: a 100W panel system (UGX 400,000-800,000) can power lights, phone charging, TV, and radio. Installation fee: UGX 50,000-100,000. Larger systems (500W-2kW) for shops, schools, and health centres: installation fee UGX 200,000-500,000. Solar water heating: growing demand in urban areas, installation UGX 300,000-800,000. Learn solar skills: free training from organisations like Solar Sister, Barefoot College partnerships, and UIRI (Uganda Industrial Research Institute).

Growing your electrical services business: 1) Build a team — hire 1-2 apprentices, train them, and take on multiple jobs simultaneously. Pay apprentices UGX 5,000-10,000/day plus lunch. 2) Partner with builders and contractors — become their go-to electrician. Offer competitive rates for repeat business. Leave your phone number on every DB board you install. 3) Get certified — Uganda Electricity Generation Company Ltd (UEGCL) and Electricity Regulatory Authority (ERA) certifications increase trust and allow you to bid on government contracts. 4) Stock commonly needed materials (cables, sockets, switches) and sell at site — markup 20-30% saves the client time and earns you extra. 5) Specialise in solar — get certified by solar companies as an authorised installer. 6) Join the Uganda Master Electricians Association for networking and tender alerts. 7) Create a WhatsApp Business profile with photos of completed work. 8) Risks to manage: electrical shock (always test before touching), falling from heights (use proper ladders), undercutting by unqualified workers (differentiate on safety and quality).
